page: Amazon Web Services t3a.large is a burstable general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides 2 virtual CPUs mapped to a single physical core with hyper-threading. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which translates to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance features no local storage and no dedicated GPU, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the t3a.large in the lower tier of cloud compute resources. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 10% category, with scores of 526.29 and 588.49 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show similar limitations, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 77.55 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 11.52 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is slightly stronger but remains weak, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 1608.74 placing it in the bottom 25%.

The burstable CPU allocation model allows the instance to handle temporary spikes in workload, but sustained high-utilization tasks will exhaust CPU credits and degrade performance. This resource profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for low-intensity workloads that require a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio without the expense of dedicated compute resources.

The t3a.large is best suited for light, non-continuous workloads. Appropriate use cases include small-scale web serving, development and testing environments, code repositories, and low-traffic database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services t3a.large is a burstable general-purpose instance utilizing the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with 2 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate that the server resides in the lower performance tier, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics, as well as memory bandwidth, ranking in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Because of its burstable CPU allocation, this instance is highly cost-efficient for workloads with variable resource demands. It is best suited for low-traffic web serving, development environments, and lightweight background tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.large is a burstable general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.large is a burstable instance with 2 vCPUs, AMD EPYC 7571 processor, and 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, suited for light workloads.
tagline: A burstable and budget-friendly x86 server featuring a balanced memory-to-core ratio designed specifically for lightweight, non-continuous, and variable workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
Equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
Burstable CPU allocation model for handling temporary workload spikes
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for development environments, code repositories, and light web serving
